gap_registry.gap_title: No Authentication / RBAC — All Modules Exposed
gap_registry.description: auth.py exists but is deferred. No session management, no role-based access, no persona-level module gating. Any URL is reachable by any user. Violates DORA, EBA ICT, GDPR Article 25 (data protection by design).
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: Cannot go live with institutional clients. Regulatory filings require audit-grade access logs. EBA Pillar 3 submissions require segregation of duties.
gap_registry.root_cause: Deferred per implementation plan to unblock feature development. No JWT middleware wired.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Wire FastAPI JWT middleware + Supabase Auth (already in stack). Implement 4 RBAC roles: admin, analyst, viewer, auditor. Gate module routes by role.

gap_registry.gap_title: No Audit Trail — Calculation Runs Not Versioned
gap_registry.description: audit_log table exists in migration 010 but is never written to. No timestamped record of: who ran what calculation, with which inputs, producing which outputs. BCBS 239 compliance requires full lineage. CSRD ESRS requires assurance-grade data lineage.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: External auditor cannot verify reported figures. CSRD limited-assurance engagement blocked. EBA supervisory review of ICAAP climate stress scenarios has no evidence trail.
gap_registry.root_cause: audit_log table created in migration 010 but no FastAPI middleware or service-layer calls write to it.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Wire audit_log writes in: (1) all POST calculation endpoints via middleware decorator, (2) scenario run completions, (3) report generation events. Store: user_id, endpoint, input_hash, output_hash, timestamp, duration_ms.

gap_registry.gap_title: Seed Data Masking Real API Gaps — False Confidence in Outputs
gap_registry.module_name: Portfolio Analytics, Climate Risk, Financial Risk
gap_registry.description: dashboardStore.js and multiple modules fall back to deterministic seeded data when the backend is offline or returns errors. The UI shows no visible warning that results are synthetic. Risk officers may confuse demo data with real client data.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: A risk analyst presenting NGFS scenario results in a credit committee meeting may unknowingly present seeded fallback data. This is a material mis-statement risk.
gap_registry.root_cause: Fallback implemented for demo resilience without a prominent 'DEMO MODE — NOT REAL DATA' visual indicator on every affected output panel.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Add a global `isFallback` banner (amber, full-width) that appears on any page rendering seeded data. Log fallback events to audit_log with `data_source: 'seed'`.

gap_registry.gap_title: IFRS 9 ECL — Missing LGD and EAD Climate Overlay at Stage 2/3
gap_registry.description: ECL engine calculates climate-adjusted PD but does not propagate climate stress into LGD (collateral value erosion under physical risk) or EAD (drawdown probability increase under transition stress). EBA GL/2022/16 §§53-58 explicitly requires all three components to be climate-sensitised.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: Stage 2 lifetime ECL is systematically understated for flood-zone collateral. Capital requirements (CET1) may be misstated by 15-30bps for high-physical-risk portfolios.
gap_registry.root_cause: ecl_climate_engine.py implements PD climate multipliers but LGD/EAD climate adjustments are placeholders returning base values.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Implement: (1) Physical risk → collateral haircut → LGD uplift (IPCC flood damage functions), (2) Transition risk → drawdown probability → EAD uplift (sector carbon cost pass-through). Add unit tests for Stage 2 ECL with flood-zone collateral.

gap_registry.gap_title: Portfolio Analytics — No Real Cross-Module Entity Linkage
gap_registry.description: Migration 042 creates cross_module_entity_linkage table and LEI FK backfills but the application layer does not use them. ECL assessments, PCAF portfolios, CSRD entity registry, and climate risk runs remain data islands. Portfolio-level climate VaR cannot be computed using real cross-module data.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: Cannot generate a unified entity-level climate profile linking: ECL stage + financed emissions + physical risk score + CSRD disclosure status. This is the core ask from Pillar 3 ESG templates (EBA ITS 2024).
gap_registry.root_cause: FK schema created but no JOIN queries in route handlers or analytics service. lineage_orchestrator.py identifies this as a known bridge gap.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Build Entity360Service that joins portfolios_pg → climate_assessment_runs → ecl_assessments → pcaf_results → csrd_entity_registry on company_profile_id/LEI. Expose via /api/v1/entity360/{lei} endpoint.

gap_registry.gap_title: NGFS Phase V Not Implemented — Modules Use Phase II/IV Mix
gap_registry.module_name: Scenario Analysis, Climate Risk, Portfolio Analytics
gap_registry.description: NGFS published Phase V scenarios (Oct 2023) with revised carbon prices, GDP impacts, and sectoral transition pathways. climate_transition_risk_engine.py has NGFS_PHASE5_SCENARIOS dict but portfolio_pg routes and scenario_analysis service still reference Phase II parameters in some embedded dictionaries.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: ECB/EBA supervisory climate stress test submissions must reference current NGFS vintage. Using Phase II carbon prices (€85/t in 2030 vs Phase V €170/t Net Zero) understates transition risk by ~50% for energy-intensive sectors.
gap_registry.root_cause: Incremental engine development — Phase V dict added to transition engine but not propagated to all consumer modules.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Create a single NGFS_SCENARIOS source-of-truth config file (JSON/YAML). Replace all hardcoded scenario dicts with imports from this file. Add version field. Run regression tests to verify all 6 modules use consistent Phase V values.

gap_registry.gap_title: SFDR PAI — Only 18 of 64 Mandatory + Additional Indicators Implemented
gap_registry.description: SFDR RTS Annex I Table 1 requires 14 mandatory PAI indicators for investments, plus 2 for sovereigns, plus 4 for RE. Table 2 lists 46 additional voluntary indicators. The current SFDRPAIPage implements ~18. Missing key indicators: PAI #4 (fossil fuel exposure), PAI #7 (non-renewable energy intensity), PAI #10 (violations of UNGC/OECD), PAI #14 (board gender diversity), all sovereign indicators.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: Article 8/9 fund SFDR annual report (PAIA) is incomplete. FCA/AMF review would flag missing mandatory indicators. Investor due diligence from LP questionnaires cannot be completed.
gap_registry.root_cause: Initial build covered most common indicators. Less-used mandatory indicators (sovereign, real estate, gender diversity) were deferred.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Map all 64 indicators to data fields. Implement the 14 missing mandatory indicators first. Add UNGC/OECD violations flag via binary input + reference list. Real estate indicators: GHG intensity per sqm, energy efficiency label.

gap_registry.gap_title: Double Materiality — No Structured IRO Assessment Workflow
gap_registry.description: DoubleMaterialityPage exists but the EFRAG DMA process is not implemented as a structured workflow. There is no: (1) IRO identification step with stakeholder input capture, (2) impact materiality scoring matrix (scale × scope × irremediability), (3) financial materiality scoring (likelihood × magnitude), (4) materiality threshold configuration, (5) output materiality statement linked to ESRS disclosure requirements.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: CSRD-in-scope companies cannot use this module to produce the EFRAG DMA-compliant materiality statement required to determine which ESRS topics to report on. This is a prerequisite for the entire CSRD disclosure workflow.
gap_registry.root_cause: Page scaffolded but EFRAG IG 1 DMA methodology (60-page process) not fully implemented. Complex multi-step workflow deferred.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Implement 5-step EFRAG DMA wizard: (1) Value chain mapping, (2) IRO long-list (using ESRS AR16 sector-specific lists), (3) Impact scoring (3×3 matrix), (4) Financial scoring (2×2 matrix), (5) Materiality threshold and ESRS topic shortlist output. Wire to csrd_materiality_topics table (migration 013).
gap_registry.resource_profile: 1 backend dev, 1 frontend dev, 1 CSRD/EFRAG expert

gap_registry.gap_title: No Real External Reference Data Integration — All Factors Hardcoded
gap_registry.description: lineage_orchestrator.py explicitly flags 8 MISSING reference datasets: WHO mortality, NatCat loss data, IPCC AR6 damage functions, Basel III NSFR/LCR ratios, FATF high-risk jurisdictions, FAO crop yield projections, EUDR deforestation criteria, BNG Metric 4.0. Emission factors (DEFRA, GHG Protocol), NGFS parameters, and NACE codes are all embedded as Python dictionaries rather than DB tables with versioning.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: Emission factors cannot be updated when DEFRA/EPA publishes annual revisions without code deployments. IPCC AR6 damage functions are unavailable, so physical risk EL calculations use simplified proxies. NatCat data missing means insurance-associated emissions are computed without real catastrophe data.
gap_registry.root_cause: Reference data embedded in code for development speed. No reference data management system (RDMS) built yet.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Phase A: Move all embedded factor tables to PostgreSQL reference_data schema with version/source/valid_from/valid_to columns. Phase B: Build scheduled jobs to pull DEFRA/EPA emission factors (annual), NGFS scenario updates (biennial), NACE codes (on EU publication). Phase C: Integrate NatCat API (Swiss Re, Munich Re) and IPCC AR6 damage function library.

gap_registry.gap_title: Nature Risk — No PostGIS; Spatial Queries Blocked
gap_registry.description: Nature risk module uses latitude/longitude float columns. PostGIS extension not enabled on Supabase. This blocks: (1) polygon-based facility footprint analysis, (2) intersection with WDPA (protected area) boundaries, (3) KBA (Key Biodiversity Area) overlap detection, (4) 30×30 conservation target analysis, (5) watershed-level water risk aggregation.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: TNFD LEAP Step L2 (Locate assets) requires geospatial intersection with biodiversity sensitivity maps. Without PostGIS, the platform can only show a point on a map — it cannot compute the actual exposure score to protected areas. TNFD v1.0 recommendation A1 explicitly requires this.
gap_registry.root_cause: PostGIS not enabled at Supabase project level. Schema uses FLOAT lat/lng instead of GEOMETRY(Point, 4326).
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Enable PostGIS extension on Supabase. Migration: ALTER TABLE nature_re_assessments ADD COLUMN geom GEOMETRY(Point, 4326); backfill via ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(lng, lat), 4326). Load WDPA + KBA + IBAT boundary layers. Implement ST_Intersects for TNFD L2 calculations.

gap_registry.gap_title: PCAF Financed Emissions — Missing Part A Asset Classes (Listed Equity, Sovereign Bonds)
gap_registry.description: PCAF Standard Part A (2022) covers 7 asset classes: Listed equity & corporate bonds, Business loans & unlisted equity, Project finance, Commercial RE, Mortgages, Motor vehicles, Sovereign bonds. The current implementation covers Business Loans and partial Project Finance. Listed equity attribution factor (market cap method) and Sovereign bond allocation factor (GDP-based) are missing.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: Investment banks with listed equity / bond portfolios cannot compute PCAF-compliant financed emissions. Scope 3 Category 15 disclosure for large banks will be incomplete. SFDR PAI #1 (GHG emissions) will be miscalculated.
gap_registry.root_cause: PCAF Part A listed equity/sovereign implementation deferred in favour of Part C (facilitated) and Part B (insurance) coverage.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Implement: (1) Listed equity: Attribution Factor = ownership% × (market_cap / (market_cap + total_debt)), (2) Corporate bonds: AF = bond_value / total_debt, (3) Sovereign: AF = investment / total_government_expenditure. Add DQS derivation for each. Wire to pcaf_portfolios table.

gap_registry.gap_title: EU ETS — Missing Article 10a Benchmark Updates (Phase IV Revised 2024)
gap_registry.description: EU ETS Phase IV free allocation uses 52 product benchmarks set in Commission Decision 2021/447. The 2024 revision updated 5 key benchmarks (hot metal, sinter, coke, lime, steam crackers). The GarEtsPage still uses 2021 benchmark values. For integrated steel producers this creates a ~8% over-allocation of allowances in calculations.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: EuroSteel AG's Henrik Müller would receive misleading free allocation forecasts. CBAM declarations filed based on these calculations could be materially incorrect, exposing the company to declarant compliance liability.
gap_registry.root_cause: Benchmark values hardcoded as Python dict. No update process when EU Commission publishes Decision revisions.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Move benchmarks to DB reference table (ets_product_benchmarks) with valid_from/valid_to versioning. Update with 2024 values. Add annual review reminder to data governance calendar.

gap_registry.gap_title: CSRD ESRS — No Facility-Level Data Granularity
gap_registry.description: All climate and emissions data is captured at entity/company level. ESRS E1 requires facility-level GHG disclosure (E1-6 §44: 'significant sources at site level'). ESRS E2 requires facility-level pollution metrics. Physical risk (ESRS E1-9) requires site-specific hazard exposure. The DB schema has no facility table.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: A pharmaceutical company with 15 manufacturing sites must report site-level GHG data. Without facility-level capture, the platform cannot support CSRD ESRS E1-6 at the required granularity, blocking assurance engagement.
gap_registry.root_cause: Platform designed around portfolio/entity paradigm. Facility-level was not in scope for initial build.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Add facilities table: (facility_id, entity_id FK, name, country, lat, lng, nace_code, floor_area_sqm, employees). Add facility_emissions junction. Update ESRS E1-6 form to support multi-facility entry with bulk CSV upload.

gap_registry.gap_title: No Time-Series Architecture — Historical KPI Trends Not Stored
gap_registry.description: csrd_kpi_values table exists (migration 013) with a reporting_year column but no TimescaleDB hypertable or partitioning. No data is written to it from any calculation service. ESRS requires year-on-year comparatives (3 years for quantitative KPIs). The platform currently cannot show trend lines for any metric.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: CSRD ESRS E1-4 §28 requires 3-year GHG trajectory. TCFD metrics reporting requires 5-year trend. Without time-series storage, users must manually re-enter historical data each year.
gap_registry.root_cause: TimescaleDB listed in plan.md but not implemented. csrd_kpi_values table created but not populated.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Phase 1: Wire all calculation endpoints to write results to csrd_kpi_values. Phase 2: Enable TimescaleDB hypertable on csrd_kpi_values(reporting_date). Phase 3: Add trend-line charts to key ESRS E1 disclosure panels.

gap_registry.gap_title: XBRL/iXBRL Export — API Endpoints Not Fully Wired to ESRS Taxonomy
gap_registry.description: XBRLPage has 4 tabs (Export, Ingestion, Completeness, Trends). The /api/v1/xbrl-export/generate endpoint exists but is not mapped to actual csrd_kpi_values data. The ESRS XBRL taxonomy published by EFRAG (v1.0, Dec 2023) has 1,144 elements. The current export generates a shell XML without real data binding.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: CSRD-in-scope companies must file iXBRL-tagged annual reports with national registers from FY2025. A generated XBRL file with no data bindings cannot be submitted. This makes the XBRL feature non-functional for compliance.
gap_registry.root_cause: XBRL feature scaffolded. Real data binding requires completing GAP-014 (time-series KPI storage) first.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Map each of the 330 IG3 quantitative DPs (migration 014) to their ESRS XBRL taxonomy element code. Build XBRL serializer that queries csrd_kpi_values and renders iXBRL. Validate output against EFRAG ESRS XBRL Taxonomy Conformance Suite.

gap_registry.gap_title: Stranded Assets — No Integration with IEA STEPS/APS/NZE Technology Pathways
gap_registry.description: Stranded asset calculator uses simplified stranded risk scores per plant type. It does not integrate IEA's World Energy Outlook 2024 asset stranding timelines (coal power: 2031 in NZE; gas power: 2037 in NZE; upstream oil: 2030 in NZE). IRENA REmap cost curves are also absent.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: NordWind AG's Lars Andersen needs IEA-consistent stranding timelines to justify write-down provisions for gas peaker plants. Without IEA pathway data, the stranded asset output cannot be used in TCFD strategy disclosure.
gap_registry.root_cause: Stranded asset engine uses proprietary simplified scores. IEA WEO data integration not planned.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Load IEA WEO 2024 asset stranding timelines as reference tables. Add scenario selector (STEPS, APS, NZE) to stranded asset calculator. Show stranding year range + probability distribution under each scenario.

gap_registry.gap_title: CBAM — Missing Definitive Period (2026+) Calculation Logic
gap_registry.description: The CBAM calculator covers the transitional period (2023-2025) reporting. The definitive period (from Jan 2026) introduces actual financial payments: CBAM certificates purchased at weekly EUA auction price, ETS price paid in country of origin deducted, accredited CBAM declarant workflow. None of this is implemented.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: EuroSteel AG and EuroAuto GmbH need to model CBAM financial exposure for 2026 onwards for capex planning and green bond prospectus disclosures. Without definitive-period logic, the calculator is a transitional reporting tool only.
gap_registry.root_cause: Transitional period implemented first as it was the immediate compliance need (2023-2025). Definitive period deferred.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Implement CBAM definitive period: (1) Certificate purchase cost = embedded_emissions × (weekly_EUA_price - origin_carbon_price), (2) Annual financial liability projection, (3) Accredited declarant registration workflow fields. Integrate weekly EUA price feed.

gap_registry.gap_title: Portfolio Analytics — No Peer Benchmarking With Real Data
gap_registry.description: PeerBenchmarkPage exists but generates all comparative data deterministically from seeds. No real peer data from CDP, Bloomberg ESG, MSCI ESG Ratings, or EBA Transparency Exercise is ingested. Benchmark rankings are synthetic.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: Investment strategy teams need real peer comparison to justify ESG tilt decisions. A synthetic peer ranking has no evidential value for regulatory filing or client reporting.
gap_registry.root_cause: Real peer data requires commercial data subscriptions (CDP €50k/year, Bloomberg ~€200k/year). Seeded as placeholder.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Phase 1: Integrate EBA Transparency Exercise data (free, public) for banking sector peers. Phase 2: CDP open data for large corporates. Phase 3: Commercial integration with MSCI/Bloomberg ESG via API.

gap_registry.gap_title: Supply Chain — SBTi FLAG Methodology Missing for Agriculture/Forestry
gap_registry.description: SBTi target-setting supports Corporate pathway (1.5°C/Well-below 2°C) for emissions. The SBTi FLAG (Forest, Land & Agriculture) methodology for land-use emissions is not implemented. For pharma, retail and automotive companies with bio-based supply chains, FLAG emissions can represent 30-50% of Scope 3.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: PharmaCo BV's Amira Hassan cannot set FLAG-compliant targets for purchased botanicals and excipients. PanEuro Retail SA's Sophie Laurent cannot show EUDR-linked deforestation-free supply chain targets.
gap_registry.root_cause: SBTi corporate pathway implemented. FLAG methodology (published 2022) is a separate framework not yet in scope.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Implement SBTi FLAG: (1) Land-use change emissions inventory (IPCC Tier 1 land categories), (2) FLAG target boundary (% removals vs % emission reductions), (3) FLAG intensity target calculation for food/forest/land companies. Add EUDR linkage for deforestation-free certification.

gap_registry.gap_title: Real Estate — CRREM Pathway Missing Country-Specific Decarbonisation Budgets
gap_registry.description: CRREM v2.0 (2023) provides country × building-type decarbonisation pathways (kgCO2/sqm/year) aligned with 1.5°C. The current implementation uses one global pathway. Missing: 28 EU country-specific pathways, 8 building types (office, retail, hotel, logistics, residential, mixed-use, healthcare, industrial), post-2030 trajectory steepening.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: PropFund AG's Julia Weber needs German-specific CRREM pathways for office and residential assets. A global pathway understates stranding risk for high-carbon German building stock and overstates it for Nordic low-carbon portfolios.
gap_registry.root_cause: CRREM v1 single pathway used as initial implementation. v2 country × building-type matrix requires structured reference data load.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Load CRREM v2.0 pathway table: (country_iso2, building_type, year, kgco2_per_sqm_budget). Build lookup in CRREM service. Add country + building_type selectors to RE assessment form. Display stranding year as intersection of asset EUI with pathway budget.

gap_registry.gap_title: Monte Carlo — No Correlated Scenario Sampling Across Risk Factors
gap_registry.description: Monte Carlo page generates independent P5/P25/P50/P75/P95 distributions per scenario. No correlation structure between: (1) physical risk frequency and transition risk carbon price, (2) GDP shock and PD migration, (3) energy price and sector EL. BCBS climate guidance requires consideration of compound and correlated climate risk events.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: Independent sampling understates tail risk for portfolios with correlated exposure (e.g., European gas-heavy utility portfolio exposed to both carbon price spike and cold winter physical risk). ICAAP capital adequacy may be understated.
gap_registry.root_cause: Simpler independent sampling implemented first. Cholesky decomposition for correlated sampling requires correlation matrix estimation from historical data.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Implement Cholesky factorisation on a user-configurable correlation matrix (6×6: carbon_price, GDP, physical_loss, energy_price, interest_rate, FX). Pre-populate with NGFS Phase V empirical correlations. Add correlated VaR output alongside existing independent VaR.

gap_registry.gap_title: Narrative Report Templates — No Dynamic Data Binding to DB
gap_registry.description: narrative_templates.py has 20 {placeholder}-style templates for TCFD, CSRD, SFDR, ISSB, SEC, GRI, BRSR, etc. These are injected as static narrative strings. There is no dynamic data binding — templates do not pull actual computed values from the DB. Reports show 'N/A' for most fields.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: A TCFD strategy disclosure section that says 'Physical risk score: N/A' is not usable for regulatory filing. The narrative template system has the right structure but generates no substantive content without data binding.
gap_registry.root_cause: Templates built as structure placeholders. Data binding requires completing GAP-014 (time-series KPI) and GAP-005 (entity linkage) first.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Build a `ReportDataBinder` service: for each template, define a data_resolver dict mapping placeholder keys to SQL queries on csrd_kpi_values + climate_assessment_runs + ecl_assessments. Call resolver before template render. Add null-check with 'Data not yet entered — please complete [Module X]' guidance links.

gap_registry.gap_title: EUDR — No Geolocation Polygon Validation for >4ha Plots
gap_registry.description: EUDR Article 9 requires geolocation data: coordinates for plots ≤4ha, GeoJSON polygon for plots >4ha. The eudr_engine.py validates point coordinates but does not validate GeoJSON polygon topology (self-intersection, winding order, closure) or cross-reference against the EU deforestation satellite monitoring data (JRC Global Forest Change layer). Cutoff date validation (31 Dec 2020) uses a simple date comparison without satellite imagery verification.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: PanEuro Retail SA needs legally defensible due diligence statements. An Article 4(2) DDS based on unvalidated polygons exposes the company to Art 12 enforcement by national competent authorities. DDS rejection rate from regulators would be high.
gap_registry.root_cause: GeoJSON polygon validation requires shapely/geodjango library + JRC satellite data API integration. Both are absent.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Integrate shapely for polygon topology validation. Connect to Global Forest Watch API (free tier) or JRC Global Forest Change layer for satellite-based deforestation verification. Add `forest_cover_change_verified: bool` field to DDS output.

gap_registry.gap_title: Sovereign Climate Risk — Missing Adaptation Finance Gap Data
gap_registry.description: Sovereign climate risk composite score uses 4 dimensions (Physical 30% + Transition 25% + Fiscal 25% + Adaptation 20%). The Adaptation dimension uses ND-GAIN scores but missing: (1) adaptation finance gap as % GDP (UNEP Adaptation Gap Report), (2) climate vulnerability index (WRI), (3) resilience infrastructure investment ratio. The 51-country profiles are static and not updated from any live source.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: Portfolio managers using sovereign climate risk for EM bond allocation decisions need current adaptation finance gap data. Static 2023 profiles may misclassify rapidly deteriorating sovereigns (Pakistan, Egypt) as lower risk than current data suggests.
gap_registry.root_cause: Static profiles embedded in service file. No update pipeline from ND-GAIN / UNEP / WRI.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Load sovereign profiles into sovereign_profiles reference table. Add adaptation_finance_gap_pct_gdp and wri_vulnerability_index fields. Schedule annual refresh from ND-GAIN CSV download + UNEP Adaptation Gap Report data.

gap_registry.gap_title: BRSR India — Missing Core + Leadership Indicator Set
gap_registry.description: BRSR module exists but only covers SEBI BRSR Core mandatory KPIs (9 indicators). BRSR 2023 amended framework adds 40+ leadership indicators covering: ESG ratings, sustainability-linked borrowings, climate transition risk, supply chain BRSR assurance, scope 3 emissions, biodiversity impact. Indian subsidiaries of global groups must now file both Core + leadership.
gap_registry.impact_on_workflows: PharmaCo BV's Indian subsidiary would fail SEBI BRSR leadership compliance if filing is based only on Core indicators. Non-compliant BRSR filing triggers BSE/NSE listing non-compliance.
gap_registry.root_cause: BRSR Core implemented first as it was the primary SEBI mandate. Leadership indicators are newer (2023) and more complex.
gap_registry.remediation_approach: Implement 40 BRSR 2023 leadership indicators. Add SEBI sector-specific guidance for top 1000 listed entities. Map to ESRS/ISSB crosswalk for global entities reporting both CSRD and BRSR.
gap_registry.resource_profile: 0.5 backend dev, 0.5 India ESG regulatory expert

immediate.rationale: Security, audit trail, and demo-data transparency are prerequisites for any client deployment. ETS benchmark update is a quick 0.5-day fix with high regulatory impact.
immediate.resource_profile: 2 backend devs, 0.5 security engineer, 0.5 ETS expert
success_metrics: All API routes return 401 for unauthenticated requests
Every calculation run writes a record to audit_log
Pages rendering seed data show amber DEMO MODE banner
ETS 2024 benchmark values validated against EC Decision 2024/XXX

short_term.rationale: ECL climate overlay completeness, NGFS Phase V consistency, SFDR PAI full set, and CBAM definitive period are all required for the next regulatory filing cycle (Q2 2026).
short_term.resource_profile: 2 backend devs, 1 quant modeler, 1 ESG regulatory expert, 0.5 data engineer
success_metrics: ECL Stage 2 LGD climate haircut ≥5% for flood-zone collateral
All 6 scenario modules return identical carbon prices for Net Zero 2030
SFDR PAI report shows all 14 mandatory indicators populated
CBAM 2026 certificate cost projection matches EC test case ±2%

medium_term.rationale: Cross-module entity linkage, facility-level data, PostGIS, time-series storage, and XBRL export are all architectural investments that unlock the full value of the existing calculation engines.
medium_term.resource_profile: 2 backend devs, 1 data engineer, 1 GIS specialist, 1 frontend dev, 1 CSRD expert, 0.5 XBRL specialist
success_metrics: Entity360 endpoint returns unified climate+ECL+CSRD profile in <500ms
ESRS E1-6 facility-level GHG input form supports 20+ sites per entity
PostGIS ST_Intersects TNFD L2 query returns WDPA overlap in <2s
csrd_kpi_values receives writes from all ESRS calculation endpoints
XBRL export passes EFRAG conformance suite validation

long_term.rationale: Real peer data integration requires commercial licensing and extended data partnership negotiations. Correlated Monte Carlo requires quant R&D.
long_term.resource_profile: 1 data engineer, 1 quant modeler, commercial data budget (€150-250k/year)
success_metrics: EBA Transparency Exercise peer data updated quarterly
Correlated VaR 99.5% differs from independent VaR by ≥15% for correlated sector portfolios
Bloomberg/MSCI ESG pilot integrated for ≥1 pilot client
